note: Maps abstract capabilities to an ORDERED LIST of providers. Skills ask for a capability (chat, docs, ...); the capability-routing convention resolves it here, fans out across every listed provider that is actually wired, and merges. A provider that isn't available at runtime (mcp server not in catalog, cli not on PATH) is skipped, so listing a not-yet-wired provider is harmless and lights up automatically once added. A capability whose providers all resolve to nothing degrades to web/files. Swap this file to retarget every data skill at once. Provider kinds: mcp (gateway server via mcp-find/add/exec, OR a local stdio server registered in mcp.json), cli (binary on PATH), http (host URL), none. Company-specific capabilities (e.g. a CRM, a warehouse, an HR directory) live in a private overlay and are not part of the public stack.

github.about: repos, PRs, issues, releases (HTTPS token injected by the sbx proxy)

gworkspace.about: Gmail, Drive, Calendar via the host-token wrapper

chat.about: Slack messages, channels, threads, users. Stdio MCP server (`pi-stack-host slack`, Go) registered with sbx (`make mcp-register`) and run by the MCP gateway; the token stays on the host. Resolves through the gateway; degrades if not registered/attached.

issues.about: Jira issues and Confluence pages (gateway catalog)
